target.title: fix(discord): keep progress summaries in status mode
target.body:
## Summary

This PR fixes Discord/channel progress drafts in status mode so they can show safe tool explanations instead of collapsing to only the generic tool label.

- Keep channel-owned tool progress callbacks flowing even when default verbose tool-progress messages are disabled.
- Let `commandText: "status"` show only operand-free exec summaries, such as `run tests`, while hiding raw command text.
- Suppress raw/precomputed command item metadata in status mode so command operands cannot bypass the status policy.
- Update docs, UI hints, generated channel metadata, and focused regression coverage for the refined status-mode contract.

Success means Discord progress drafts can show useful safe command summaries during tool calls, while paths, URLs, tokens, script names, grep patterns, package script operands, and unknown git/openclaw subcommands still collapse to the generic exec label.

Reviewers should focus on the status-mode privacy boundary and the channel-owned progress forwarding condition.

## Linked context

No linked issue.

Requested from a real Discord-backed fleet where progress drafts were visible, but status mode showed only the tool label instead of the safe command explanation.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Discord progress drafts in `commandText: "status"` mode showed only the tool label when explain-mode command detail was active, even for safe command summaries such as test/build commands.
- Real environment tested: Private Discord-backed OpenClaw fleet running a Git-source gateway build with progress drafts enabled and `commandText: "status"`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Rebuilt the Git-source gateway, restarted it, verified gateway health, and ran a Discord turn that emitted an exec tool start with explain-mode command detail under status-mode progress.
- Evidence after fix: The Discord progress draft showed the safe summary form, `run tests`, instead of only the generic exec label. Focused SDK tests also confirm grep operands, URLs, script paths, package script operands, unknown git/openclaw subcommands, raw fallback commands, cwd suffixes, token-bearing commands, and command item metadata all collapse to the generic exec label.
- Observed result after fix: Safe operand-free command summaries remain visible in progress drafts; raw command text and command-derived operands remain suppressed.
- What was not tested: The full repository test suite was not run locally; focused tests and build passed.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: No public screenshot is attached because the live behavior proof used a private Discord fleet server.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Before the fix, the same status-mode progress draft showed the exec tool label without the command explanation.

## Tests and validation

Commands run:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugin-sdk/channel-streaming.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.process.test.ts -t "command progress"`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts`
- `pnpm config:channels:check`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm build`
- `codex review --base origin/main`

Regression coverage added or updated:

- Status-mode command summaries now cover safe summaries, cwd stripping, raw fallback suppression, token/path/URL/script/package-script operand suppression, unknown git/openclaw subcommands, and raw command item metadata suppression.
- Discord progress processing now verifies status-mode command progress renders the safe summary.
- Auto-reply dispatch now verifies channel-owned progress callbacks still forward when verbose summaries are off.

Known pre-fix failure:

- Status-mode Discord progress drafts could omit the safe command explanation and show only the generic tool label.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? `Yes`

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? `No`

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? `Yes`

Highest-risk area: accidentally exposing raw command operands in status-mode progress.

Risk mitigation: status mode now uses a strict allowlist of operand-free summaries, hides raw command text, and suppresses raw/precomputed command item metadata. Focused tests cover sensitive command shapes and known bypass paths.

## Current review state

Next action: Maintainer review.

Waiting on: CI and maintainer review.

Bot/reviewer comments addressed: Local `codex review --base origin/main` reported no actionable correctness issues after the privacy-boundary fixes.

AI assistance: This PR was prepared with Codex and locally reviewed with `codex review --base origin/main`.
target.author: abnershang
